http_server_requests_seconds_bucket and _count metrics are exposed
on the actuator/prometheus endpoint.

test client, and configure Redis ObjectMapper with JavaTimeModule for
proper date serialization.

Fix neo4j container startup failure caused by read-only volume mount

Neo4j 5 chowns everything under /var/lib/neo4j/ on startup, which
fails on the :ro mounted init-graph.cypher. The file is already
mounted separately in the neo4j-init sidecar container.

Implement news feed system with fanout architecture

Spring Boot 3.4 / Java 21 app with Nginx load balancer, Post Service
(PostgreSQL + Redis cache), Fanout Service (RabbitMQ → workers → News Feed
Cache), Neo4j graph DB for friendships, Prometheus/Grafana monitoring,
load test client, unit tests, and Docker Compose orchestration.

commit.message:
feat: add password-only authentication with session management

Single-user auth using Argon2id password verification against an env var
(AUTH_PASSWORD_HASH) and server-side sessions with SHA-256 hashed tokens
stored in a Session table. Includes login page, auth/CSRF middleware,
rate limiting, sliding 30-day session expiration, and a CLI script
(pnpm hash-password) to generate the password hash.

Add MMS support — Ayden can see images sent via text

and passes to Claude vision API as image content blocks. Supports JPEG,
PNG, GIF, and WebP. Image-only messages (no text) are handled. Image
attachments noted in conversation history.

commit.message:
Fix Heisenberg auto-move compatibility with Arduino potentiometers

The old deadband compared the mapped pot position against the moving
selector, so any animation movement triggered the deadband and killed
auto-move immediately. apply_pots also unconditionally overwrote sq_cx/sq_cy,
snapping the selector back to the pot position every 50 ms.

Fix: deadband now compares current vs previous RAW pot values. Stationary
pots never override the selector position, so auto-move runs freely. Only
actively-turned pots stop the animation and take absolute control of X/Y.
The size pot always updates the slider regardless of animation state.

commit.message:
Fix ticker: add CSS transition so text changes are visible, 10s -> 5s

Without `transition: opacity 0.4s ease` on the element, the opacity
snapped instantly — the text changed but was invisible for only 400ms,
making it appear stuck on the first item. Also matched the setTimeout
delay to the transition duration (400ms) and halved the cycle interval.

Keratoconus, CL Param) to update only the result div on input instead

and digit reordering issues.

Also: linked Park's 3-step in CN assessment table to the Parks tool.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Allow comparisons between different widths and signedness

Comparison operators (==, !=, <, <=, >, >=) no longer require operands
to have the same type. Both operands are extended to a common type
before comparing: signed if either is signed, with an extra bit when
mixing signedness to preserve the unsigned range. This mirrors how
arithmetic already handles mixed operands and eliminates a common source
of boilerplate casts in hardware verification code.

commit.message:
Replace hyphens with underscores in build artifact naming (#14)

* Fix binary naming to use underscores for download-release-binary compatibility

The download-release-binary action expects assets named {name}_{os}_{arch}
(underscore-separated), but build-go.yml was producing {name}-{os}-{arch}
(hyphen-separated). This changes the separator to underscores so releases
are compatible with the action.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013hrSaZARGeURfo2Rrcwb4z

* Replace custom build-go workflow with go-toolchain action

Use wow-look-at-my/go-toolchain@v1 which handles Go setup, building,
testing, and cross-compilation. This removes the need for the custom
build-go.yml reusable workflow and ensures binaries are named with the
underscore convention (dats_linux_amd64) expected by download-release-binary.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013hrSaZARGeURfo2Rrcwb4z

* Fix go-toolchain validation: add tests to meet 80% coverage threshold

go-toolchain auto-fixed test assertions (manual if-checks to
assert/require calls) and introduced a testify dependency. Fixed the
resulting compile error (duplicate ok := declaration).

Added comprehensive tests for runner/ and cmd/ packages to bring
total coverage from 8.3% to 80.4%, meeting the go-toolchain minimum.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013hrSaZARGeURfo2Rrcwb4z

* Rename Go module from bats-declarative to dats

The go-toolchain action derives the binary name from the Go module path.
With the old module name (github.com/mhaynie/bats-declarative), binaries
were produced as bats-declarative_linux_amd64 etc., which didn't match
the upload artifact glob (build/dats_*) or the download-release-binary
naming convention.

Renamed to github.com/wow-look-at-my/dats so go-toolchain produces
dats_linux_amd64, dats_darwin_arm64, etc.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013hrSaZARGeURfo2Rrcwb4z

* Remove symlinks before artifact upload

go-toolchain matrix creates dats_host and dats symlinks in build/.
These match the dats_* glob and shouldn't be included in release
artifacts. Delete all symlinks from build/ before uploading.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013hrSaZARGeURfo2Rrcwb4z

---------

fix(docker): add ARG/ENV for VITE_API_URL in frontend Dockerfile

frontend to not know the API endpoint.

commit.message:
fix: remove double dispatch in discussions bot

The discussions.js task handler was dispatching agentic-lib-workflow.yml
when action=request-supervisor, AND the bot workflow's dispatch-supervisor
job was also dispatching it. This caused two workflow runs per bot
interaction. Remove the dispatch from discussions.js — the bot workflow's
dispatch-supervisor job is the single authority for this.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refine homepage intro and simplify research card titles

Add "sits at the intersection of systems and ML" to intro. Revert card
titles to shorter forms (Learning Algorithms and Systems, Model Serving
and Inference, Agentic Systems) and tighten card descriptions.

Release v0.3.0: /design-system, /map-systems, status line, UPGRADING guide (#2)

* Add UPGRADING.md migration guide and link from README

Covers v0.1→v0.2 upgrade with three strategies (git merge, cherry-pick,
manual copy), file safety categories, and post-upgrade verification steps.
Structured to support future version sections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename /design-systems to /map-systems + /design-system and fix all references

Split the monolithic /design-systems skill into two focused skills:
- /map-systems: systems decomposition and index creation
- /design-system: guided section-by-section GDD authoring

Updated all cross-references across 14 files: README, UPGRADING,
WORKFLOW-GUIDE, quick-start, skills-reference, game-concept template,
systems-index template, brainstorm, design-review, gate-check,
project-stage-detect, setup-engine, and start skills. Fixed skill
counts from 36 to 37 everywhere. Added /map-systems and /design-system
to quick-start Paths A and B workflows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix cross-reference gaps, broken hooks, and stale workflow chains

- Fix log-agent.sh parsing agent_type instead of agent_name (always logged "unknown")
- Fix GDD status lifecycle: design-system now writes Approved/Designed/In Review
- Clean up settings.local.json vestigial Bash grants from development
- Delete orphaned docs marked for removal in UPGRADING.md
- Add /design-system to next-steps in /start, /brainstorm, /setup-engine
- Fix WORKFLOW-GUIDE: add /map-systems + /design-system to Appendix C Workflow 1
- Fix invalid /map-systems map argument in WORKFLOW-GUIDE Step 2.1
- Update map-systems frontmatter to document [system-name] argument
- Update commit hook to validate all 8 required GDD sections (was 5)
- Update README template count 28 → 29, add 5 missing templates to quick-start

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add custom status line with 7-stage production pipeline

Introduces a status line showing context %, model name, and production
stage at a glance. Aligns gate-check and project-stage-detect to a
unified 7-stage model (Concept → Systems Design → Technical Setup →
Pre-Production → Production → Polish → Release). Stage is determined
by explicit override (production/stage.txt) or auto-detected from
project artifacts. Epic/Feature/Task breadcrumb appears conditionally
in Production+ stages via a structured STATUS block in active.md.

commit.message:
feat: セッション完了後に3秒遅延で自動開始 - Issue #111

セッション完了時に3秒後に次のセッションを自動開始する機能を実装

### 変更内容
- `sessionCompletePending` フラグを追加して、セッション完了を管理
- セッション完了時に `changeSessionTypeWithPending` を使ってフラグを `true` に設定
- 別のeffectでフラグを検知して、3秒後に自動開始
- `start()` アクションの中でフラグを `false` に設定

fix: security and reliability fixes for PIX checkout and scheduler

- Sanitize pixId input with regex validation and URL encoding
- Fix webhook/polling Redis key mismatch by storing under both pixId and email
- Add idempotency guards to webhook and CompleteRegistration
- Handle time.LoadLocation error with UTC-3 fallback in processor
- Restore startup task execution lost during cron migration
- Use request context instead of context.Background in stats controller
- Stop frontend PIX polling on error to prevent infinite retries
- Add tests for dual-key storage, idempotency, and SetIdempotencyFlag

Add staleness check for hygiene report in weekly-review

Parse the Generated timestamp from the hygiene report and warn if
it's >20h old. Covers the natural "hygiene tonight, review tomorrow
morning" pattern while catching stale data from previous cycles.
